title: OpenAI Build Week Submission
goal: Prepare, demonstrate, document, and submit Keynu as a strong OpenAI Build Week project built with Codex before the July 21 submission deadline.
currentMilestone: Define the winning submission concept and minimum competition-ready demo
completedMilestones: Recognized Keynu as the candidate project for OpenAI Build Week
Confirmed that Keynu Mission Control can persist and restore the active mission across ChatGPT connections
nextMilestones: Audit the current working Keynu capabilities that can be demonstrated without speculative rebuilding
Choose one clear competition story and user problem
Build and verify the minimum competition-ready demo
Prepare the demo script and recording
Prepare submission description, evidence, and repository documentation
Complete and verify the submission before the deadline
rules: Treat OpenAI Build Week as the highest-priority active mission until the submission is completed or the user explicitly changes the active mission.
Every autonomous KAP job must directly advance the OpenAI Build Week submission or remove a demonstrated blocker to it.
Do not resume general Mission Control dashboard development unless that work is required for the competition demo or submission.
Prefer using and demonstrating existing verified Keynu capabilities over creating unrelated architecture or broad new features.
Use Codex materially in the project workflow and preserve clear evidence of what Codex helped build, because the challenge is for projects built with Codex.
Use Node.js-first architecture and prefer native Node.js APIs.
Use PowerShell only when Node.js cannot perform the required operation.
Use small independently verifiable KAP jobs.
Read back every created or modified file.
Run relevant TypeScript builds and tests after integration changes.
Do not claim completion without matching KAP REPORT evidence.
Preserve the active Keynu browser connection unless a verified restart is required.
Do not overwrite unrelated working-tree changes.
After every 10 KAP exchanges, provide the user with a concise progress summary covering accomplishments, verification results, remaining blockers, and the next submission-critical action.

architectureDecisions.title: Build Week is the active mission
architectureDecisions.summary: Mission selection must keep all autonomous reasoning and execution focused on producing a verified competition submission rather than continuing lower-priority product work.

architectureDecisions.title: Existing capabilities before speculative expansion
architectureDecisions.summary: The submission should first assemble a compelling demonstration from verified Keynu Mission Control, KAP, BrowserAgent, continuation, runtime, driver, memory, and evidence capabilities.

architectureDecisions.title: Repository-backed mission memory
architectureDecisions.summary: The Build Week objective, rules, milestones, and next actions must be restored from repository-managed mission files rather than relying on one ChatGPT conversation.

architectureDecisions.title: Evidence-driven completion
architectureDecisions.summary: A feature, demo step, or submission asset is complete only when runtime output, tests, readback, or another concrete artifact verifies it.

recommendedReading.title: OpenAI Build Week official page
recommendedReading.reason: Defines the challenge context, requirement to build with Codex, key dates, and submission expectations.

recommendedReading.title: KAP 1.0 specification
recommendedReading.reason: Required to understand the verified AI-to-runtime workflow used in the demo.

recommendedReading.title: Mission Continuation Engine
recommendedReading.reason: Explains how Keynu prevents silent stopping and maintains autonomous progress.

recommendedReading.title: Repository memory ADR
recommendedReading.reason: Explains how Keynu restores project context across AI conversations.

recommendedReading.title: Development protocol
recommendedReading.reason: Defines mandatory verification and safety practices for AI-assisted Keynu development.

knownLimitations: The local .keynu mission registry may override the repository registry and must be synchronized before reconnecting BrowserAgent.
The final competition concept, judging narrative, demo scope, and submission assets have not yet been selected or completed.
The repository must preserve clear evidence of meaningful Codex usage for the Build Week project.
The remaining schedule is short, so unrelated refactoring and broad feature expansion are out of scope unless they unblock the demo.

nextActions.title: Synchronize and activate the Build Week mission locally
nextActions.reason: The local .keynu registry can override the repository and must point to openai-build-week before BrowserAgent reconnects.

nextActions.title: Audit verified Keynu capabilities for the competition demo
nextActions.reason: The strongest submission should be based on working features and concrete evidence rather than assumptions.

nextActions.title: Select one clear Build Week submission story
nextActions.reason: The demo, implementation, documentation, and video need one understandable user problem and value proposition.

nextActions.title: Build and verify the minimum competition-ready demo
nextActions.reason: A reliable end-to-end demonstration is more important than general product expansion before the deadline.

nextActions.title: Prepare and verify all submission assets
nextActions.reason: The final repository, demo video, description, evidence, and submission form must be ready before July 21.
